title: Simulating fermions with a digital quantum computer | Nature Reviews Physics
why:
- Simulating physical systems with fermions is classically challenging, and quantum computers offer tools to study such systems through quantum simulation algorithms.
- The simulation of fermionic systems on qubit-based quantum computers requires encoding the antisymmetric exchange of fermions into qubits.
- First and second quantization provide different methods for encoding fermionic degrees of freedom into qubits, each with specific strengths and approaches based on system requirements.
- Studying quantum systems that are beyond the capabilities of classical computers has been identified as one of the most important applications of quantum computing technology.
